title: question about eva mass production model
display_title: question about eva mass production model
wikitext:
[]: question about eva mass production model             Quote Post by kuramomotaro » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:47 am ¿how can eva mass production model sees without eyes? thx

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Aug 09, 2014 2:51 am Likely some kind of sonar or other form of mechanical feedback.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Aug 09, 2014 3:01 am In all honesty, the Mass Produced Evas probably are blind, given their slow reflexes during their battle with Unit-02. The eyes on the rest of the Evas provide visuals for the pilots inside, but the MPEs run on a Dummy Plug and have no pilots so the eyes may have been unnecessary.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Aug 09, 2014 10:06 am They don't have eyes because where we're going we don't need eyes to see.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Aug 09, 2014 11:18 am Possibly sonar or radar, or some other form of detection that does not require visible organs. After all, weirder Angels like Ramiel and Leliel were able to detect things without having obvious sensory organs - something similar to whatever they were using might have been installed in the MP Evas. Chuckman wrote: They don't have eyes because where we're going we don't need eyes to see.

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Sat Aug 09, 2014 12:49 pm I've always assumed they used their AT Field to detect things in their immediate area or had hidden cameras plastered everywhere. I mean, those things are huge, it wouldn't be that hard to put some small cameras somewhere and not have them visible. Or they're guided by Plot Navigation Systems.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Aug 09, 2014 12:52 pm Cameras would work perfectly well, - but it begs the question about why they don't have eyes in the first place, or at least exposed eyes. Either they lack eyes altogether due to the weird shape of their head, or they have something else to substitute for the eyes, or their eyes are covered by the armor on purpose.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Aug 09, 2014 1:04 pm A suitably high-resolution radar does it for me.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Aug 09, 2014 1:13 pm Radar does have its limitations, after all. The Russians have the habit of fitting infrared/optical/laser suites onto their latest fighters precisely to counter the American superiority in stealth aircraft, which makes such a thing rational to incorporate into a "realistic" solution to the lack of eyes. Having the MP Evas with a high-resolution radar, sonar, and a full infrared/optical/laser suite would be awesome, but somehow I think that's not the case (more out of intuition than reason, admittedly).

[]:             Quote Post by kuramomotaro » Sat Aug 09, 2014 4:09 pm thanks for all of your answers the eva mass production model in effect is a dummy system and technically not need eyes , but even the machines need cameras or sensors, if the EVAS is based in the biology of the angels, maybe his "eyes" not have a conventional form...(¿the "marks" in her heads? ) on other side i re-watched the battle of the mass model eva's, and the inside of his head was exposed revealing a really big "brain" in comparison..and remember the seele side have been made this evas far beyond the limitations of a pilot, so ¿how about of some manner of "location" using that giant brain? ¿psychic?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 09, 2014 5:53 pm Related thread -- http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/679/Windows-To-The-Soul/ Relevant quote therefrom Mr. Tines wrote: Like the Pinball Wizard, they probably play by sense of smell...

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sat Aug 09, 2014 6:11 pm It's nice to see The Who are still relevant. I don't have any problem in them being blind and simply relying on all other senses; no radar or sonar. It serves to make them more animalistic and somewhat more grotesque - in a way.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Aug 09, 2014 8:55 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Cameras would work perfectly well, - but it begs the question about why they don't have eyes in the first place, or at least exposed eyes. Either they lack eyes altogether due to the weird shape of their head, or they have something else to substitute for the eyes, or their eyes are covered by the armor on purpose.

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Mon Aug 11, 2014 11:14 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Keep in mind that the Evangelion units don't really need to have anything that look like eyes- hell, they don't even need to have a human shape.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Aug 11, 2014 12:58 pm While I admit is the most obvious in the NTEverse, my opinion is that it's also shown in Episode 19.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Aug 11, 2014 3:24 pm I would argue that they're in part held in their human shape by the armor/restraints. Even if it's only a human arm that Yui regrows, when the restraints come off and the Eva has a power source it shows an ability to shapeshift. It would probably be more difficult to pilot a non-humanoid Eva, since piloting involves a degree of familiarity with the shape of the Eva. The pilot walks by walking, etc. People don't have laser blast or hovering muscles, so there should be some kind of a learning curve. If you're piloting the God-eva you can cheat.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Aug 11, 2014 3:29 pm Or if you're so synched up to it that you become it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 11, 2014 4:12 pm They're human-shaped because they're clones of humans, simple as that. As for how they sense their surroundings, Firetrout has the right of it IMO. And Ursus, there's no question-begging here. I am disappoint. You should know better!

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Aug 11, 2014 4:47 pm Bagheera wrote: They're human-shaped because they're clones of humans, simple as that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 11, 2014 5:04 pm Chuckman wrote: I don't see any treevangelions anywhere.
